title: Fix moe-agent.ps1 temp file cleanup (B34)
description:
**B34 (LOW): Temp MCP config files never cleaned up**
- File: scripts/moe-agent.ps1:216
- $mcpConfigFile created in %TEMP% but never deleted on exit or error. Accumulates stale files.
- Fix: Add a try/finally block around the main script body, or register a cleanup trap. In the finally block: `Remove-Item -Path $mcpConfigFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue`. Also check moe-agent.sh for the same issue.
definitionOfDone: moe-agent.ps1 cleans up temp MCP config file on exit
moe-agent.sh cleans up temp file if applicable
Cleanup works on both normal exit and Ctrl+C

implementationPlan.description: Fix moe-agent.ps1 temp file cleanup. In scripts/moe-agent.ps1, wrap the main polling loop (~line 640 onwards) in a try/finally block. In the finally block add: `if ($mcpConfigFile -and (Test-Path $mcpConfigFile)) { Remove-Item -Path $mcpConfigFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }`. Also register a Ctrl+C handler using `Register-EngineEvent PowerShell.Exiting -Action { ... }` or use `[Console]::CancelKeyPress` to clean up on interrupt. Error handling: SilentlyContinue ensures no errors on already-deleted files.
implementationPlan.note: Wrapped main do-while loop in try/finally. Finally block removes $mcpConfigFile with -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue. Handles normal exit, errors, and Ctrl+C.

implementationPlan.description: Fix moe-agent.sh temp file cleanup. In scripts/moe-agent.sh, check if it creates a temp MCP config file. If so, add a trap handler: `trap 'rm -f "$MCP_CONFIG_FILE"' EXIT INT TERM`. Place this right after the temp file is created. The EXIT trap ensures cleanup on both normal exit and signals. Error handling: rm -f never fails on missing files.
implementationPlan.note: No changes needed. moe-agent.sh already has `trap cleanup_temp EXIT` (line 90) for its SECURE_TEMP_DIR. The MCP config in the shell script is written to ~/.config/claude/mcp_servers.json (permanent config), not a temp file.

implementationPlan.description: Verify: Run moe-agent.ps1 with a test project and Ctrl+C. Verify the temp file in %TEMP% is deleted. Check that no moe-mcp-config-*.json files accumulate.
implementationPlan.note: Verified ps1 try/finally structure is syntactically correct. Daemon build and 327 tests pass. Cannot run interactive agent script in CI but structure is standard PowerShell try/finally pattern.
